text.title: handoff-id-payload Failure Modes
text.summary: Four failure modes with symptom, root cause, and repair.

section.title: Antipatterns

antipattern.description: Passing the prior conversation as part of the handoff "in case the next agent needs it".
antipattern.reason: Almost never needed; when it is, fetching by task_id costs the same and is reversible. Pre-emptive forwarding scales O(n²).

section.p: Symptom: each downstream agent's context grows with chain length. Root cause: defensive payload. Fix: trim payload to `{task_id, target_agent, decision_metadata}`; let agents fetch on demand.

antipattern.description: Using the handoff payload as the only source of task state with no shared store.
antipattern.reason: Re-runs and audit trails become impossible; the system has no memory between agents besides what was passed in the last hop.

section.p: Symptom: failed handoff cannot be retried; debugging the chain is impossible. Root cause: missing store. Fix: stand up a shared store keyed by task_id; have agents read on first action and append events on completion.

antipattern.description: Supervisor returns a free-form message naming the next worker ("next: writer").
antipattern.reason: Model picks worker names that do not exist; orchestrator needs to re-prompt or fall back; routing becomes fragile.

section.p: Symptom: routing failures spike when supervisor model changes. Root cause: parsing free text. Fix: force `SupervisorDecision` structured output with `target_agent` as a Literal enum.

antipattern.description: Worker overwrites task state on completion (`store.put(task_id, output)`).
antipattern.reason: Prior input is lost; retries on downstream failure cannot recover; audit trail is destroyed.

section.p: Symptom: failed downstream stage cannot be replayed because the upstream output is gone. Root cause: overwrite semantics. Fix: switch to `append_event(task_id, event)`; the latest state is computed by replaying events.
